value.reason: The cited page documents the AGENTS.md feature as a **project-scoped instruction file** mechanism — a layered file-discovery system that feeds instructions into Codex before each task. This is precisely the **adjacent capability** labeled "Project-scoped instruction file," not the target capability of "Output & system-prompt customization." While AGENTS.md files do influence agent behavior, the page's entire focus is on the file-discovery hierarchy, layering, and project/global scoping — not on shaping a persona, output style, or system prompt in a general-purpose way. The feature documented is a structured instruction-file pipeline tied to directory scope (git root, subdirectories, global home), which matches the "Project-scoped instruction file" adjacent capability that the target capability must be distinct from. Therefore: documents_capability = false (page covers an adjacent capability, not the target one), label_ok = false (AGENTS.md is the name of the project-scoped instruction file feature, not an output/system-prompt customization feature), and description_ok = false (the description accurately describes AGENTS.md's mechanics but misattributes it to the wrong capability category).
